items.title: Match the NB variant to the feature encoding
items.avoid: Calling `MultinomialNB` on binary vectors or `BernoulliNB` on raw count matrices without conversion.
items.why: Each implementation assumes a specific generative story; mismatched inputs produce miscalibrated likelihoods.
items.example: Pipeline: `CountVectorizer()` → `MultinomialNB()` for reviews; `CountVectorizer(binary=True)` → `BernoulliNB()` for short chat utterances.

items.title: Always score in log space mentally
items.avoid: Multiplying many probabilities in raw space where underflow silently zeroes scores.
items.prefer: Think in log posteriors: log P(y) + Σ log P(x_i|y); libraries do this internally.
items.why: Documents have hundreds of features; product of probabilities underflows float64 without logs.
items.example: Ten factors of 0.01 yield 1e-20 in raw space; log sum ≈ 10·log(0.01) stays representable.

items.title: Preprocess text consistently between train and test
items.avoid: Different tokenization, casing, or vocabulary between training and deployment.
items.prefer: Fit vectorizer on train only; reuse the same pipeline for inference with identical preprocessing.
items.why: NB has no mechanism to recover from vocabulary drift—unknown tokens map to smoothed but weak evidence.
items.example: If training lowercases but production keeps "Free" vs "free" as distinct tokens, θ estimates split across duplicates.
